description_long: This object is a large, heavy-duty firearm with a multi-barrel rotary design, reminiscent of a Gatling or minigun-style shotgun. The main body is robust and metallic, featuring three long, parallel barrels with visible ribbing and a cylindrical housing at the rear, likely for ammunition or power. The rear section includes a prominent handle and a trigger assembly, with additional mechanical components and red accent details visible along the body. The overall construction is industrial, with a matte black and metallic finish, and the design suggests it is intended for high-capacity, rapid-fire use. The object appears to be constructed primarily from metal, with some plastic or polymer elements in the handle and trigger area. Its imposing size and configuration make it suitable for use in urban defense or heavy-duty tactical scenarios.
description: A large, multi-barrel rotary shotgun with a robust metallic body, three long barrels, and a prominent handle and trigger assembly. The design is industrial and heavy-duty, featuring red accents and a matte black finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the three long barrels, cylindrical rear housing, and prominent handle with red accents.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular arrangement of the three barrels and the open muzzle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same three-barrel configuration, handle, and mechanical details.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the handle, trigger assembly, and the back of the cylindrical housing.
attribute_barrel_configuration: rotary multi-barrel configuration with three parallel barrels, each featuring ribbing along their length
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with a simple, unreinforced design
